title: Inconsistency in Verilog Synthesis: Yosys Successfully Synthesizes Code That Fails in Vivado and Quartus Due to Syntax Errors

features.name: power_operator_between_registers
features.description: Code should include a power operator (**) with two register variable operands, which violates synthesis restrictions that require the left operand to be a constant or parameter

features.name: chained_power_operators
features.description: Code should include chained power operator expressions where one power operation is the operand of another power operation, creating nested exponentiation

features.name: power_with_unary_operator_operand
features.description: Code should include a power operator where the right operand is a unary logical negation expression, which some synthesis tools reject

features.name: power_in_arithmetic_comparison
features.description: Code should include power operator expressions used as operands in comparison operations, which may be rejected by synthesis tools
features.snippet:
reg22 <= (reg17 - wire9) == (reg16 ** reg18);
reg21 <= (reg16 ** reg18) == (wire11 - wire8);

features.name: power_in_subtraction_expression
features.description: Code should include power operator expressions used in subtraction operations, which some synthesis tools reject
features.snippet:
reg19 <= (reg16 ** reg18) - (reg17 - wire9);
reg22 <= (reg20 - wire9) - (reg16 ** reg18);
